questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to beat the red light
The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Stop and wait for a green arrow
questions.explanation: A steady yellow signal warns that the light is changing to red, so you should stop if it is safe rather than trying to race through.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red eight-sided octagon, a shape used for no other sign so it can be recognized instantly.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light require you to do?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely, then proceed when it is safe
Stop and wait for the light to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: come to a full stop and go only when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light means you should:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a full stop and yield to all traffic
Treat it the same as a red light
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light warns of a hazard or intersection ahead, so reduce speed and continue carefully.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangular sign mean?
questions.explanation: A red and white downward triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road mean?
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is not permitted because it is unsafe at that location.

questions.prompt: Orange traffic signs and cones usually indicate:
questions.explanation: Orange is the standard color for construction and maintenance work zones, alerting drivers to slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow shown with a red light allow you to do?
choices: Proceed in the direction of the arrow after yielding as required
Make a U-turn in any direction
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected right-of-way to move in the direction it points, after yielding to any pedestrians or vehicles still in the intersection.

questions.prompt: A white painted line separating lanes of traffic indicates:
choices: Traffic moving in the same direction
The edge of a one-way street only
questions.explanation: White lane lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: When you approach an intersection where the traffic signals are completely dark and not working, you should:
choices: Treat it as an all-way stop
Wait for an officer to arrive
questions.explanation: When signals are out, every driver must stop and proceed in turn as if the intersection were controlled by stop signs.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: When two drivers reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you want to make a left turn at an intersection, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: In Washington, when may you turn right at a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
Only when an arrow is green
questions.explanation: Right turns on red are allowed in Washington after stopping completely and yielding, unless a posted sign prohibits the turn.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling over to the right and stopping until they have passed.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks at intersections.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Stop and wait for all traffic to clear
Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
questions.explanation: Vehicles already circulating in a roundabout have the right-of-way, so entering drivers must yield before merging in.

questions.prompt: When you reach an intersection at the same time as a vehicle on a through highway while you are on a smaller side road, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle on the through highway
Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: Drivers entering or crossing a through highway from a lesser road must yield to traffic already on the main road.

questions.prompt: A left turn on a red light is permitted in Washington only when:
choices: Turning from a one-way street onto another one-way street
A passenger tells you it is clear
questions.explanation: Washington allows a left turn on red only from a one-way street onto another one-way street, after stopping and yielding.

questions.prompt: When you are turning and a pedestrian is crossing the street you are turning into, you should:
choices: Wait and let the pedestrian finish crossing
questions.explanation: Turning drivers must yield to pedestrians in the crosswalk of the street they are entering.

questions.prompt: Before merging onto a freeway, you should:
choices: Stop at the end of the ramp
Match the speed of freeway traffic and merge into a gap
Expect traffic to slow for you
questions.explanation: Use the on-ramp to accelerate to the speed of traffic and merge smoothly into an available gap, yielding to vehicles already on the freeway.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the maximum speed limit on city and town streets in Washington?
questions.explanation: Washington's default speed limit on city and town streets is 25 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What is the default maximum speed limit on Washington county roads unless otherwise posted?
questions.explanation: Unless otherwise posted, the default maximum speed on county roads in Washington is 50 mph.

questions.prompt: What following distance does the three-second rule help you maintain?
choices: A safe gap behind the vehicle ahead
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you keep enough space behind the car in front so you have time to react and stop.

questions.prompt: When the road is wet or visibility is poor, you should:
choices: Drive at the posted speed limit
Reduce your speed below the posted limit
Increase your speed to clear the area
questions.explanation: Posted limits apply to ideal conditions, so you must slow down when rain, snow, or poor visibility reduce traction and sight distance.

questions.prompt: What is the default maximum speed limit on Washington state highways unless otherwise posted?
questions.explanation: The statutory default maximum on Washington state highways is 60 mph, though many are posted higher or lower.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: The road is dry and clear
You are being tailgated or conditions are poor
You are in the left lane
questions.explanation: Extra following space gives you a buffer when conditions are bad or a tailgater reduces the space behind you.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Can block traffic and cause crashes
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly disrupts the flow of traffic and can lead to rear-end collisions and unsafe passing.

questions.prompt: The maximum speed limit on rural interstate highways in Washington can be as high as:
questions.explanation: Rural interstate freeways in Washington may be posted at speeds up to 70 mph.

questions.prompt: What does 'basic speed law' mean?
choices: You may always drive the posted limit
You must drive at a speed reasonable and safe for conditions
You must always drive 5 mph below the limit
Speed limits only apply at night
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires you to drive at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for current road, weather, and traffic conditions, even if it is below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: To stop safely, you should leave enough space ahead because stopping distance increases with:
questions.explanation: The faster you drive, the longer it takes to stop, so higher speeds require greater following and stopping distances.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking both your mirrors and your blind spot ensures no vehicle is beside you before you move over.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is intended primarily for:
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right, and the left lane is meant for passing, not for cruising.

questions.prompt: You should not pass another vehicle when:
choices: You are on a hill or curve with limited sight distance
The road is straight and clear
questions.explanation: Passing is unsafe and illegal where you cannot see far enough ahead, such as on hills and curves.

questions.prompt: When a school bus is stopped with its red lights flashing and stop arm out, you must:
choices: Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children and wait until the red lights turn off and the stop arm retracts.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to the right lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to safely return to the right lane.

questions.prompt: When passing another vehicle, you should:
choices: Exceed the speed limit if needed
questions.explanation: You must complete a pass without exceeding the posted speed limit; if you cannot pass legally, wait for a better opportunity.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line on your side of the road means:
choices: You may pass when it is safe
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates passing is allowed when the road ahead is clear and it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: What should you do if another driver is trying to pass you?
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly and stay in your lane
questions.explanation: Help the passing driver by holding a steady speed or easing off slightly so they can complete the pass safely.

questions.prompt: A center lane marked with two-way left-turn arrows is used for:
choices: Making left turns from either direction
questions.explanation: A center two-way left-turn lane is shared by vehicles from both directions for making left turns and is not for through travel or passing.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist in Washington, you should give them:
choices: A safe distance of at least three feet
questions.explanation: Drivers must pass bicyclists at a safe distance, generally at least three feet, to avoid endangering them.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
choices: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small size makes it easy to overlook, so check carefully, especially at intersections and when changing lanes.

questions.prompt: The large areas around a truck where the driver cannot see you are called:
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots, or no-zones, and if you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you must:
choices: Yield and stop, as they may be blind
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a pedestrian who may be visually impaired, and drivers must yield and stop for them.

questions.prompt: When a truck makes a right turn, it may:
choices: Swing wide and need extra room
questions.explanation: Large trucks often swing wide to complete turns, so never try to squeeze between a turning truck and the curb.

questions.prompt: When driving near children playing or in a school zone, you should:
choices: Slow down and be ready to stop
Expect children to watch for you
questions.explanation: Children can dart into the road unexpectedly, so reduce speed and stay alert near schools, parks, and residential areas.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on the roadway are:
choices: Required to ride on sidewalks only
Entitled to use a lane like other vehicles
Required to yield to all cars at all times
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the right to use the road and may occupy a full lane when needed for safety.

questions.prompt: To avoid a crash with a motorcycle at an intersection, you should:
choices: Look twice and judge the motorcycle's speed and distance
Turn quickly in front of it
questions.explanation: Because motorcycles are small and their speed is hard to judge, take a second look before turning across their path.

questions.prompt: In Washington, what is the legal blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit for adult drivers age 21 and over?
questions.explanation: It is illegal to drive in Washington with a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher if you are 21 or older.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 21 in Washington, what BAC can result in a DUI under the zero-tolerance law?
questions.explanation: Washington's zero-tolerance law makes it illegal for drivers under 21 to drive with a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Washington's E-DUI law, holding a cell phone while driving is:
choices: Illegal, even when stopped at a red light
Allowed if you use one hand
questions.explanation: The E-DUI law prohibits holding a personal electronic device while driving, including when stopped in traffic or at a red light.

questions.prompt: What does Washington's implied consent law mean?
choices: You agree to chemical testing if arrested for DUI
You can refuse all tests without penalty
Police need a warrant for everything
You consent to vehicle searches anytime
questions.explanation: By driving in Washington you give implied consent to breath or blood testing if lawfully arrested for DUI, and refusing leads to license suspension.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers of commercial vehicles in Washington?
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers in Washington are held to a stricter limit and may not drive with a BAC of 0.04 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It impairs judgment, coordination, and reaction time
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow your reactions and impair the judgment and coordination needed for safe driving.

questions.prompt: The best way to avoid driving while impaired is to:
choices: Plan ahead for a sober ride
questions.explanation: Only time removes alcohol from your body, so arrange a sober driver, rideshare, or other plan before you drink.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving is dangerous mainly because it:
choices: Takes your eyes, hands, and attention off driving
questions.explanation: Texting combines visual, manual, and mental distraction, making it one of the most dangerous things you can do while driving.

questions.prompt: Prescription or over-the-counter medications can affect driving by:
questions.explanation: Many legal medications can impair driving by causing drowsiness or slowing reactions, so check labels and warnings before driving.

questions.prompt: Under the E-DUI law, what limited phone use is generally allowed while driving?
choices: Holding the phone to read texts
A minimal single touch to start or end a hands-free function
questions.explanation: The law permits only the minimal use of a finger to activate or end a hands-free feature on a mounted device, not holding the phone.

questions.prompt: In Washington, when must you turn on your headlights in relation to your windshield wipers?
choices: Whenever your wipers are running continuously
questions.explanation: Washington law requires headlights to be on whenever conditions require continuous use of your windshield wipers.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Low beams cut through fog better, while high beams reflect off the moisture and reduce your visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
questions.explanation: To recover from a skid, take your foot off the gas and steer gently in the direction you want the front of the car to go.

questions.prompt: Headlights are required in Washington during what time period at night?
choices: From a half hour after sunset to a half hour before sunrise
questions.explanation: Washington requires headlights from a half hour after sunset until a half hour before sunrise, and whenever visibility is reduced.

questions.prompt: Why are bridges and overpasses especially dangerous in cold weather?
choices: They freeze before other road surfaces
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and freeze before regular roads, creating hidden ice.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy rain, you should:
choices: Increase speed to clear the area
Slow down and increase your following distance
questions.explanation: Wet roads reduce traction, so slowing down and leaving extra space helps you stop safely and avoid hydroplaning.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning happens when:
choices: Tires lose contact with the road and ride on a film of water
questions.explanation: At higher speeds on wet roads, tires can ride up on a layer of water and lose grip, causing a loss of steering and braking control.

questions.prompt: If you must stop because of very poor visibility, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on your hazard lights
questions.explanation: If conditions force you to stop, get fully off the roadway and use your hazard lights so others can see you.

questions.prompt: When sun glare makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Use your sun visor and slow down
questions.explanation: Reduce speed and use your visor or sunglasses to manage glare so you can keep a clear view of the road.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the car will roll back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an illegal place to park?
questions.explanation: Parking within an intersection or crosswalk blocks traffic and pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into traffic, you should:
choices: Check for passing cars and bicyclists
Open it only on the left
questions.explanation: Always look for approaching vehicles and bicyclists before opening your door to avoid causing a crash.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for people with disabilities only if:
choices: You display a valid disability permit or plate
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces are reserved for vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: How close to a fire hydrant are you generally prohibited from parking?
choices: You may park right next to it
Within the distance posted or marked, typically about 15 feet
Only if a fire truck is present
questions.explanation: Parking too close to a fire hydrant is prohibited so firefighters can reach it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When you leave your parked vehicle, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake and remove the key
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and removing the key helps prevent the vehicle from rolling or being stolen.

questions.prompt: A curb painted red usually means:
questions.explanation: A red curb generally indicates no parking or stopping, often near fire lanes or hydrants.

questions.prompt: When parallel parking, you should:
choices: Signal, pull alongside the space, and back in slowly
questions.explanation: Proper parallel parking means signaling, lining up with the car ahead, and backing in slowly while watching for traffic.

questions.prompt: In Washington, what is the minimum age to apply for an instruction permit if you are enrolled in driver education?
questions.explanation: Teens enrolled in an approved driver education course may apply for an instruction permit at age 15.

questions.prompt: How long must a teen hold a Washington instruction permit before getting an intermediate license?
questions.explanation: Applicants under 18 must hold the instruction permit for at least six months before getting an intermediate license.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a Washington teen complete before licensing?
questions.explanation: Teens must complete 50 hours of supervised driving, including 10 hours at night, before getting an intermediate license.

questions.prompt: While driving with an instruction permit in Washington, who must be in the front seat with you?
choices: A licensed driver who has held a license for at least 5 years
questions.explanation: A permit holder may drive only with a licensed driver who has had a license for at least five years in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with a Washington intermediate license, nighttime driving is restricted between:
questions.explanation: Intermediate license holders may not drive between 1 a.m. and 5 a.m. unless supervised by a licensed driver age 25 or older.

questions.prompt: During the first six months with a Washington intermediate license, passenger restrictions limit passengers who are:
choices: Under age 20 and not immediate family
questions.explanation: For the first six months, intermediate drivers may not carry passengers under 20 unless they are immediate family members.

questions.prompt: What is the passing score on the Washington knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the knowledge test questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: A supervising driver who certifies a teen's practice hours in Washington must have held a license for at least:
questions.explanation: The driver supervising a teen's required practice hours must be licensed and have held a license for at least three years.

questions.prompt: If a teen is not enrolled in driver education, the minimum age to apply for a Washington instruction permit is:
questions.explanation: Teens not enrolled in a driver education course must be at least 15 and a half to apply for an instruction permit.

questions.prompt: Seat belt enforcement in Washington is a:
choices: Primary offense, so you can be stopped just for not buckling up
questions.explanation: Washington's seat belt law is a primary offense, meaning an officer may stop and cite you solely for not wearing a seat belt.

questions.prompt: Children in Washington must use an approved car seat or booster seat until they:
questions.explanation: Washington requires children to use a car seat or booster until they are 4 feet 9 inches tall, then a properly fitted seat belt.

questions.prompt: Under Washington's Move Over Law, when you cannot change lanes away from a stopped emergency vehicle with warning lights, you must:
choices: Slow to at least 10 mph below the posted speed limit
questions.explanation: If you cannot safely move over, the Move Over Law requires you to slow to at least 10 mph under the posted limit when passing.

questions.prompt: If you refuse a lawful breath or blood test after a DUI arrest in Washington, you face:
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing chemical testing results in an automatic license suspension separate from any criminal penalty.

questions.prompt: A first-offense E-DUI (handheld phone) ticket in Washington carries a fine of at least:
questions.explanation: A first E-DUI offense for holding a device while driving carries a fine of at least $136, which increases for repeat offenses.

questions.prompt: Traffic violations committed by a Washington intermediate license holder can:
questions.explanation: Tickets and violations can extend a teen's intermediate-license restrictions and delay full driving privileges.

questions.prompt: Who in a vehicle is responsible for being properly restrained under Washington law?
questions.explanation: Washington law requires all occupants to be properly restrained, and children must use age- and size-appropriate restraints.

questions.prompt: Reckless driving and DUI in Washington can result in:
choices: Fines, license suspension, and possible jail
questions.explanation: Serious offenses such as DUI and reckless driving can lead to heavy fines, license suspension, and even jail time.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you are generally required to:
choices: Stop, give information, and render aid if needed
Drive to a police station only
Wait a week to report it
questions.explanation: The law requires drivers in a crash to stop, exchange information, and help injured people, and to report serious crashes.

questions.prompt: A red triangular sign pointing downward means:
questions.explanation: The downward red triangle is the yield sign, requiring you to slow and give the right-of-way before proceeding.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Allow a greater following distance than for a car
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly, so allow extra following distance to give yourself time to react.

questions.prompt: On a freeway in an urban area, the default maximum speed limit in Washington is often:
questions.explanation: Many urban freeways in Washington are posted at 60 mph, lower than the 70 mph allowed on some rural interstates.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection with no signs or signals, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle on your right
Stop and wait for a signal
questions.explanation: Without signs or signals, yield to the vehicle on your right when you reach the intersection at about the same time.

questions.prompt: Which is a safe way to handle a phone call while driving in Washington?
choices: Hold the phone to your ear
Use a hands-free or mounted device
questions.explanation: The E-DUI law allows hands-free use, so set up calls and navigation on a mounted device rather than holding your phone.

questions.prompt: You may not pass another vehicle when approaching:
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited near intersections and railroad crossings where it is unsafe and visibility may be limited.
